{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Business Management & PMO Manager",
  "description": "KPMG is a global network of independent professional services firm with deep expertise in the provision of audit, tax and advisory services to clients in various industries and sectors of the economy. The Firm is well represented in Nigeria and across the African continent, with the objective of providing exceptional and quality services to multinational, regional and local clients and to enhance the product offerings in certain previously under-serviced markets.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Management &amp; PMO Manager

Job Location: Lagos
Job type: Full time

Job Summary


	As the Business Management &amp; PMO Manager, you will lead the Group Project Management Office (PMO), drive strategic execution across business units, coordinate enterprise-wide initiatives, monitor business performance, and ensure the timely delivery of key projects through effective governance, reporting, and stakeholder engagement.


Key Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Engineering, Business, Project Management, or a related discipline (Master&#39;s degree is an advantage).
	Minimum of 8 years of experience in strategy execution, PMO, management consulting, or a similar role.
	PMP, PRINCE2, or equivalent project management certification is preferred.
	Proven experience leading enterprise projects and coordinating cross-functional strategic initiatives.
	Proficiency in Power BI, Microsoft Excel, and Microsoft Office Suite, with excellent analytical and executive reporting skills.
	Excellent stakeholder management, presentation, and communication skills, with the ability to drive execution across multiple business units.
	Experience leveraging AI productivity tools to enhance reporting and business performance is an added advantage.


Method of Applictaion&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: chinelo.egbunine@ng.kpmg.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Business Management & PMO Manager Job at KPMG




        
    

        
        KPMG is a global network of independent professional services firm with deep expertise in the provision of audit, tax and advisory services to clients in various industries and sectors of the economy. The Firm is well represented in Nigeria and across the African continent, with the objective of providing exceptional and quality services to multinational, regional and local clients and to enhance the product offerings in certain previously under-serviced markets.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Management & PMO Manager

Job Location: Lagos
Job type: Full time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	As the Business Management & PMO Manager, you will lead the Group Project Management Office (PMO), drive strategic execution across business units, coordinate enterprise-wide initiatives, monitor business performance, and ensure the timely delivery of key projects through effective governance, reporting, and stakeholder engagement.


Key Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree in Engineering, Business, Project Management, or a related discipline (Master's degree is an advantage).
	Minimum of 8 years of experience in strategy execution, PMO, management consulting, or a similar role.
	PMP, PRINCE2, or equivalent project management certification is preferred.
	Proven experience leading enterprise projects and coordinating cross-functional strategic initiatives.
	Proficiency in Power BI, Microsoft Excel, and Microsoft Office Suite, with excellent analytical and executive reporting skills.
	Excellent stakeholder management, presentation, and communication skills, with the ability to drive execution across multiple business units.
	Experience leveraging AI productivity tools to enhance reporting and business performance is an added advantage.


Method of Applictaion 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Transport Supervisor",
  "description": "SPIE Oil &amp; Gas Services (part of the SPIE Group) provides a complete range of services to some of the world&rsquo;s largest oil and gas companies through its network of offices in 25 countries across Africa, the Middle-East and Asia-Pacific.

Our turnover has more than doubled in the last few years thanks to the dedication of our 4,000 employees to whom we give training, recognition, and genuine opportunities for career development. In order to support this growth, SPIE Oil &amp; Gas Services is always seeking talented individuals to join its teams.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Transport Supervisor

Job Reference ID: 2026-44002&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
Job Location: Lagos

Job description


	We are currently looking for Transport Supervisor to join its work force in Nigeria.


Responsibilities are as follows
Activites:
Transport Operations Execution:


	Coordinate and supervise daily dispatch of vehicles in line with approved requests
	Validate journey plans and ensure full compliance before execution
	Monitor fleet positioning and availability in real time
	Resolve operational bottlenecks and escalate critical constraints.


Driver Supervision &amp; Workforce Management:


	Plan driver schedules, rotations, and shift coverage
	Monitor punctuality, attendance, and discipline
	Enforce code of conduct and driving standards
	Conduct driver briefings, coaching, and performance follow-up
	Manage welfare matters to sustain industrial harmony


Health, Safety &amp; Environment (HSE) Management:
Enforce strict compliance with:


	Defensive driving standards
	Journey management protocols
	Speed and fatigue management policies


Conduct regular:


	Toolbox talks
	Safety briefings
	On-the-spot inspections
	Participate in accident/incident investigations and corrective action follow-up Promote a &ldquo;safety-first, zero incident&rdquo; culture


Fleet Monitoring &amp; Performance Control:


	Track driver behaviour via telematics (speeding, harsh braking, route deviation)
	Monitor fleet utilization and identify inefficiencies
	Track downtime and ensure rapid turnaround
	Support optimization of vehicle allocation and route planning


Profile:
Maintenance &amp; Asset Care:


	Ensure pre-trip and post-trip inspections are completed
	Liaise with workshops for preventive and corrective maintenance
	Monitor vehicle condition and roadworthiness
	Flag recurring technical issues and recommend improvements


Fuel Management &amp; Operational Cost Control:


	Monitor fuel issuance and consumption trends
	Detect anomalies, abuse, or inefficiencies
	Promote fuel-efficient driving practices
	Support inventory control for fuel distribution


Reporting &amp; Documentation:
Maintain up-to-date records on:


	Vehicle utilization
	Driver performance
	Fuel consumption
	Maintenance activities
	Provide structured daily/weekly reports to Head of Transport
	Escalate operational risks, safety issues, and performance gaps


HSE Objectives


	Familiarize self with Company emergency procedures, carry out assigned responsibilities safely and report any incident, unsafe act or operation.
	Participate in all HSE committee meetings and all safety drills.


Qualifications / Experience Required





	Good University Degree in Finance, Economics or Engineering discipline
	At least 5 years of post NYSC working experience.
	Good command of English language.
	Good interpersonal skills.
	Knowledge of computer software like MS Office Suit, Ms Project, Primavera, AutoCAD.
",

  "validThrough": "2026-08-08",

Transport Supervisor Job at SPIE Oil & Gas Services




        
    

        
        SPIE Oil & Gas Services (part of the SPIE Group) provides a complete range of services to some of the world’s largest oil and gas companies through its network of offices in 25 countries across Africa, the Middle-East and Asia-Pacific.

Our turnover has more than doubled in the last few years thanks to the dedication of our 4,000 employees to whom we give training, recognition, and genuine opportunities for career development. In order to support this growth, SPIE Oil & Gas Services is always seeking talented individuals to join its teams.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Transport Supervisor

Job Reference ID: 2026-44002   
Job Location: Lagos
     

            
    



Job description


	We are currently looking for Transport Supervisor to join its work force in Nigeria.


Responsibilities are as follows
Activites:
Transport Operations Execution:


	Coordinate and supervise daily dispatch of vehicles in line with approved requests
	Validate journey plans and ensure full compliance before execution
	Monitor fleet positioning and availability in real time
	Resolve operational bottlenecks and escalate critical constraints.


Driver Supervision & Workforce Management:


	Plan driver schedules, rotations, and shift coverage
	Monitor punctuality, attendance, and discipline
	Enforce code of conduct and driving standards
	Conduct driver briefings, coaching, and performance follow-up
	Manage welfare matters to sustain industrial harmony


Health, Safety & Environment (HSE) Management:
Enforce strict compliance with:


	Defensive driving standards
	Journey management protocols
	Speed and fatigue management policies


Conduct regular:


	Toolbox talks
	Safety briefings
	On-the-spot inspections
	Participate in accident/incident investigations and corrective action follow-up Promote a “safety-first, zero incident” culture


Fleet Monitoring & Performance Control:


	Track driver behaviour via telematics (speeding, harsh braking, route deviation)
	Monitor fleet utilization and identify inefficiencies
	Track downtime and ensure rapid turnaround
	Support optimization of vehicle allocation and route planning


Profile:
Maintenance & Asset Care:


	Ensure pre-trip and post-trip inspections are completed
	Liaise with workshops for preventive and corrective maintenance
	Monitor vehicle condition and roadworthiness
	Flag recurring technical issues and recommend improvements


Fuel Management & Operational Cost Control:


	Monitor fuel issuance and consumption trends
	Detect anomalies, abuse, or inefficiencies
	Promote fuel-efficient driving practices
	Support inventory control for fuel distribution


Reporting & Documentation:
Maintain up-to-date records on:


	Vehicle utilization
	Driver performance
	Fuel consumption
	Maintenance activities
	Provide structured daily/weekly reports to Head of Transport
	Escalate operational risks, safety issues, and performance gaps


HSE Objectives


	Familiarize self with Company emergency procedures, carry out assigned responsibilities safely and report any incident, unsafe act or operation.
	Participate in all HSE committee meetings and all safety drills.


Qualifications / Experience Required





	Good University Degree in Finance, Economics or Engineering discipline
	At least 5 years of post NYSC working experience.
	Good command of English language.
	Good interpersonal skills.
	Knowledge of computer software like MS Office Suit, Ms Project, Primavera, AutoCAD.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

Accountant (Banking) Job at Mecer Consulting Limited




        
    

        
        Mecer Consulting Limited provides business and management, consulting, general training, etc.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accountant (Banking)

Job Location: Kubwa, Abuja (FCT) 

Job Summary


	The Accountant will manage financial records, prepare reports, monitor income and expenditure, support audits, and ensure accurate documentation of all transactions.


Key Responsibilities
     

            
    




	Prepare financial reports for management and maintain accurate accounting records.
	Record financial transactions and maintain savings, loan and repayment records.
	Perform bank reconciliations and ensure proper filing of financial documents.
	Support internal controls, audit activities and compliance checks.
	Monitor outstanding loan balances, defaults, income and expenditure.
	Support budgeting, financial planning and statutory/cooperative reporting requirements.


Qualifications and Experience


	Degree or HND qualification in Accounting, Banking & Finance, Economics, Business Administration or a related field.
	5 - 7 years of accounting experience, preferably in a cooperative, microfinance, financial institution or similar environment.
	ICAN, ANAN, ACCA or related professional certification is an added advantage.
	Must have a Banking experience.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send a copy of their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply
